category_title: Adolescent with Down's syndrome exhibiting verbal outbursts to escape demands or gain tangibles
minimal_context: Dennis is sitting in the cafeteria at the beginning of lunch while the teacher places his preferred food item in sight but out of reach.
dialogue:
TUTOR: (Sits next to Dennis and places his preferred food item in sight but out of reach) You can have this in a minute.

STUDENT: (Reaches toward the food item) No.

TUTOR: We just need to wait a little bit.

STUDENT: (Gestures at the food) Stop it.

TUTOR: Almost time.

STUDENT: That's enough! No!

options.A: Adopt a gentle, fun, facilitative approach, commenting on the food item and laughing with the student.
options.B: Ask if things were tough at home last night and be more careful with the student during lunch.
options.C: Provide immediate access to the preferred food item for 60 seconds contingent upon the challenging behavior.
options.D: Use a request for clarification to prompt the student to explain his verbal outbursts during the meal.
